summary: Tested mobile button state machine flow in OrderScreen.js - all 5 test cases passed. Verified Estado Inicial (PRE-CUENTA blue), Estado Edición (ANULAR red with counter), Estado Cierre (FACTURAR primary), Regla de Interrupción (returns to PRE-CUENTA not FACTURAR), and ENVIAR visibility.
passed_tests: Estado Inicial: PRE-CUENTA button visible (blue styling) when no items selected and no pre-cuenta printed - PASS
Estado Edición: ANULAR button visible (red styling) with counter '(1)' when item(s) selected - PASS
Estado Cierre: FACTURAR button visible (primary/orange styling) after printing pre-cuenta - PASS
Regla de Interrupción: From FACTURAR state, selecting item shows ANULAR, deselecting returns to PRE-CUENTA (not FACTURAR) - PASS
ENVIAR button always visible when pending items exist, independent of button state - PASS
Mobile viewport 375x750: All button transitions work correctly - PASS
Session storage persists 'closing' state for table - PASS
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile button state machine implemented in OrderScreen.js lines 89-112 (state definition), 327-345 (useEffect for editing transitions), 647-650 (closing state on pre-check print), 1378-1427 (mobile buttons rendering). States: 'initial' (PRE-CUENTA), 'editing' (ANULAR), 'closing' (FACTURAR). Regla de Interrupción returns to 'initial' after deselecting items from closing state. Session storage key: mobile_btn_state_{tableId}. Test tables: Mesa 3 and Mesa 5. Login: Admin PIN 0000.

regla_interrupcion.description: After selecting/deselecting from closing state, returns to initial (PRE-CUENTA) not closing (FACTURAR)
regla_interrupcion.implementation: useEffect at lines 327-345 sets state to 'initial' when selectedItems goes from >0 to 0

enviar_button.condition: pendingCount > 0, always visible regardless of mobileButtonState
